title: Feasibility and outcomes of the DNA Screen nationwide adult genomic screening pilot
methods: next-generation sequencing (Illumina NextSeq550, Agilent SureSelect XTHS2)
data_sources: Prospective participant cohort (DNA Genotek ON-600 saliva samples)
Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) Census (CALD definition, SEIFA, ARIA+ remoteness)
core_conclusion: Australia's DNA Screen prospective pilot enrolled 10,263 young adults (18-40 years) nationwide and detected pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants (PLPVs) in 2.0% of participants across ten high-risk genes linked to hereditary breast/ovarian cancer, Lynch syndrome, and familial hypercholesterolaemia. Of those referred for clinical care, 97.9% accepted and 87.3% attended specialist appointments; critically, 74.5% of attendees would have been ineligible for any government-funded criteria-based genomic testing, demonstrating that population-wide screening reaches a largely hidden at-risk population that current referral criteria miss entirely.
why_it_matters: First prospective nationwide population genomic screening pilot in a national public healthcare system; 74.5% of carriers missed by existing criteria-based pathways; high public engagement (>30,000 registrants from one day of media); diverse cohort including CALD and remote participants; provides evidence base for cost-effective preventive genomics policy.
why_nature: Landmark prospective design contrasting all prior retrospective/biobank studies; direct policy relevance for national screening programme design; equity-centred recruitment of underrepresented groups; translational bridge from clinical genetics to proactive public health delivery.
added_value_to_my_research: 2SFCA/R2SFCA spatial accessibility of 11 partner clinical genetics sites -> identify cold spots and telehealth priorities
Mobile phone mobility data -> predict referral drop-off risk by postcode for targeted outreach
Explainable ML on postcode demographics + gene panel yield -> GeoAI-for-genomics XAI model
Link PLPV carrier locations to urban environmental exposure layers -> gene-environment interaction geography
Map digital health literacy and trust scores onto CALD postcode distribution -> equity-focused screening outreach targeting
